name: Shrimp Lo Mein
instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a rolling boil over high heat. Add the spaghetti and cook, stirring occasionally, until al dente (tender but firm to the bite), about 12 minutes. Drain through a colander and set aside.

2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the chicken broth, white sugar, hoisin sauce, soy sauce, and cornstarch until fully combined and the sugar dissolves. Set the sauce aside.

3. Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat until shimmering. Add the shrimp and minced garlic, and cook, stirring frequently, until the shrimp turn bright pink and opaque throughout, about 5 minutes.

4. Reduce the heat to medium-low. Pour in the prepared sauce and add the chopped green onions to the shrimp mixture. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens and coats the shrimp with a glossy sheen, 2 to 3 minutes.

5. Add the cooked spaghetti to the skillet and toss everything together using tongs or two forks to evenly coat the noodles in the sauce. Serve immediately while hot.
simpleInstructions:
1. Cook spaghetti until al dente, then drain and set aside.

2. Whisk broth, sugar, hoisin, soy sauce, and cornstarch until smooth.

3. Stir-fry shrimp and garlic in hot oil until pink and opaque.

4. Add sauce and green onions, cook until thick and glossy.

5. Toss noodles with shrimp mixture until coated, then serve hot!
